topic.name: U.S. Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: This cluster focuses on amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States regarding the temporary suspension or modification of duties on various goods.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain clock movements.
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on artificial flowers of man-made fibers assembled as clips.
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain women's footwear with outer soles and uppers of rubber or plastics and valued over $6.50 but not over $12 per pair.
Document 4: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on 2-Methyl-4-methoxy-6-methylamino-1,3,5-triazine.
Document 5: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to: (1) modify the article description for cerium sulfide pigments; and (2) extend the temporary suspension of duty on such article.
Document 6: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on 2-Methyl-5-nitrobenzenesulfonic acid.
Document 7: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on modified aliphatic amine mixtures containing benzyl alcohol, formaldehyde, polymer with 1,3-benezenedimethanamine and phenol, 1,3-benzenedimethanamine, phenol, 4,4' -(1-methylethylidene)bis-, polymer with 2-(chloromethyl)oxirane, reaction products with ethylene-diamine.
Document 8: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on acrylic or modacrylic filament tow.
Document 9: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on magnesium zinc aluminum hydroxide carbonate coated with stearic acid.
Document 10: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on Strong Base Anionic Resin-Quaternary amine styrene divinylbenzene copolymer in the chloride form.

topic.name: Medicaid and Medicare Regulations
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative acts related to the regulation, enforcement, and expansion of Medicaid and Medicare programs in the United States.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to ensure that service providers and suppliers that have operated in one state but are excluded from participation in the Medicare program are unable to participate in other federal health care programs in another state.
Requires the Secretary to allow greater integration between Medicare databases.
Document 2: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to require Medicaid drug coverage of drugs prescribed for treatment of obesity.
Document 3: Prohibits federal payment under Medicaid for nonemergency services furnished by providers whose participation in Medicaid, Medicare, or CHIP has been terminated.
Requires a state to exclude from Medicaid participation any provider that has been terminated under CHIP.
Document 4: Increases criminal penalties for both felony and misdemeanor fraud under SSA titles XVIII (Medicare) and XIX (Medicaid).
Requires the HHS Inspector General to implement mechanisms for the sharing of information about suspected fraud.
Document 5: Requires each entity offering a health benefits plan to make available to enrollees and potential enrollees specified information, including covered items and services, a list of limitations and restrictions.
Document 6: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to provide the enhanced federal medical assistance percentage (FMAP) to every state that expands Medicaid coverage.
The bill retains this enhanced FMAP, but bases it on a term of years rather than on specific dates.
Document 7: Authorizes coverage under the Medicaid program for services at a non-hospital freestanding birth center.
Document 8: Extends Medicaid payment parity with Medicare for primary care services furnished under the Medicaid program.
Document 9: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to permit local public agencies to act as Medicaid enrollment brokers.
Document 10: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to continue through 2015 the use of a three-month quarter EHR reporting period to demonstrate meaningful use.

topic.name: Tax Legislation and Reforms
topic.description: The documents discuss various amendments and reforms to the Internal Revenue Code related to tax treatment for corporations, alternative minimum tax provisions, and employee stock ownership plans.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exempt certain distributions by S corporations to an employee stock ownership plan (ESOP) from penalty tax
Amends the Small Business Act to allow a majority-owned ESOP business concern to qualify for loans.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend nonrefundable personal tax credits against regular and alternative minimum tax liability
Increases the alternative minimum tax refundable credit amount for individuals.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code with respect to the tax treatment of S corporations
Allows S corporations to increase passive investment income from 25% to 60%.
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent the 100% exclusion from gross income of gain from the sale of certain small business stock
Allows a deduction for health insurance premiums in computing net earnings from self-employment income.
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exempt certain distributions by S corporations to an employee stock ownership plan (ESOP) from penalty tax
Modifies certain ESOP stock ownership rules.
Document 6: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to eliminate the alternative minimum tax on corporations
Applicable for taxable years beginning after 2006.
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to reduce the rate of the income tax on corporations to 0%
Eliminates the alternative minimum tax on corporations.
Document 8: Increases the alternative minimum tax exemption amount to $100,000 for married couples
Adjusts exemption amounts for inflation.
Document 9: Extends increased alternative minimum tax exemption amounts through 2007
Offsets nonrefundable personal tax credits against regular and AMT liability.
Document 10: Makes permanent and increases the alternative minimum tax exemption amount to $100,000
Adjusts increased exemption amounts for inflation.

topic.name: Veterans Health and Employment Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving healthcare and employment opportunities for veterans and their families.

Document 1: Health Care for Family Caregivers Act of 2009 authorizes medical care for caregivers of disabled veterans.
Prohibits caregivers from being subject to fees for such care.
Document 2: Veterans Employment Act of 2012 prioritizes qualified veterans for employment in the VA.
Requires annual reports on veteran hiring in the competitive service.
Document 4: Veterans Healthcare Commitment Act of 2009 prohibits recovery of costs for service-connected disability care.
Ensures veterans receive necessary medical services without financial burden.
Document 6: Caring for Veterans with Traumatic Brain Injury Act of 2009 establishes a committee for veterans with TBI.
Focuses on treatment and rehabilitation needs of veterans with traumatic brain injury.

topic.name: Education Funding and Reform
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving funding and reform initiatives for elementary and secondary education in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009 to allocate and award funds to outlying areas under the State Fiscal Stabilization Fund program
supports their funding of elementary, secondary, and postsecondary education
Document 2: authorizes a grant program to enable elementary schools, secondary schools, or specified entities that serve low-performing schools
improve the academic achievement of elementary and secondary school students
Document 3: Prohibits state or local educational agencies from requiring or permitting the Pledge of Allegiance or national anthem to be recited or sung in any language other than English
Withholds federal funds from state or local educational agencies that violate such prohibition

topic.name: Student Loan Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at reforming federal student loan policies, including interest rates, repayment plans, and loan forgiveness.

Document 1: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to extend the 3.4% interest rate on Federal Direct Stafford loans.
Loans first disbursed to undergraduate students between July 1, 2011, and July 1, 2015.
Document 2: Revises the process for enrolling a borrower in federal student loan repayment plans.
Department of Education may use the tax information of certain delinquent borrowers.
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of Education to provide student loan forgiveness under the Federal Family Education Loan, Direct Loan, and Perkins Loans programs.
Limits the maximum amount of such loan forgiveness to $2,000 after the first year.
Document 4: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require postsecondary educational lenders to disclose to borrowers at least 30 days before the first fully amortized payment on their loan is due.
Requires postsecondary educational lenders to disclose to borrowers who become 30 days delinquent on their loan.
Document 5: Allows borrowers to consolidate loans as Federal Direct Consolidation Loans.
Sets the interest rate on Federal Direct Consolidation Loans at 4%.
Document 6: Establishes a program to refinance outstanding federal student loans for borrowers of Direct Loan program loans.
Waives tuition and fees for eligible students at community colleges.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Education to establish a program to refinance the unpaid principal, accrued unpaid interest, and late charges on loans.
Sets the interest on refinanced Consolidation Loans at the rate on Federal Direct PLUS Loans.
Document 8: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to set the annual interest rate on Direct Stafford loans and Direct Unsubsidized Stafford loans.
Caps the annual interest rate on Direct PLUS loans at the rate on high-yield 10-year Treasury notes plus 4.6%.
Document 9: Requires institutions of higher education to provide student loan counseling to new borrowers and cosigners.
Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to require information on the effect of death, disability, or inability to engage in any substantial gainful activity.
Document 10: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to replace several existing federal student loan programs with a single repayment plan.
Establishes the Income Dependent Education Assistance (IDEA) Loan Program.

topic.name: Wilderness Area Designation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the designation and management of wilderness areas and components of the National Wilderness Preservation System in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Colorado Wilderness Act of 1993 to add specified lands in Colorado managed by the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) to the Powderhorn Wilderness.
Designates specified lands in Colorado as wilderness and as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Document 2: Amends the Washington Park Wilderness Act of 1988 to authorize the Secretary of the Interior to adjust the boundaries of the North Cascades National Park and the Stephen Mather Wilderness.
Requires that the boundary adjustments be such that equal acreage amounts are exchanged between the Wilderness and the Park.
Document 5: Amends the Oregon Wilderness Act of 1984 to designate certain land in Siskiyou National Forest as the 'Copper Salmon Wilderness.'
Document 6: Designates certain federal land in Oregon as the Devil's Staircase Wilderness and as a wilderness area for inclusion in the National Wilderness Preservation System.

topic.name: Drug Regulation and User Fees
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts related to the regulation of drugs, including user fees, safety, and access to medications.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to assess and collect fees related to generic drugs.
Requires the Secretary to report to Congress on the progress of the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) in achieving specified safety, access, and transparency goals.
Document 2: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the CDC to study strategies for preventing and treating the use of synthetic recreational drugs.
The Department of Health and Human Services must coordinate with the Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA) to establish and maintain a database of synthetic recreational drugs.
Document 3: Directs the Commissioner of Food and Drugs (FDA) to take actions necessary to modify the approval of controlled-release oxycodone hychloride.
Applies any such modification to drugs introduced into interstate commerce 180 days after enactment of this Act.
Document 4: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require that priority review vouchers are transferable to sponsors of human drug applications.
Prohibits such vouchers from being redeemed to affect the review of a pending human drug application.
Document 5: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to extend the authority of the FDA to collect animal drug user fees.
Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to adjust the total revenue amounts for FY2015 and subsequent fiscal years for inflation.
Document 6: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to permit an investigational drug to be made available for expanded access.
Establishes a procedure for accelerated approval of an investigational drug that is reasonably likely to predict clinical benefit.
Document 7: Transfers marijuana from schedule I to schedule II of the Controlled Substances Act.
Provides that no provision of the Controlled Substances Act shall prohibit a physician from prescribing or recommending marijuana for medical use.
Document 8: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to establish a reciprocal marketing approval process for drugs approved in other countries.
User fees apply to requests for reciprocal marketing approval.
Document 9: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to establish the Center for Postmarket Evaluation and Research for Drugs and Biologics.
Requires the Director to conduct postmarket risk assessment and surveillance of FDA-approved drugs.
Document 10: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to apply debarment provisions to any drug products.
Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to consider public health in determining the appropriateness and period of debarment.

topic.name: FISA Amendments and Reforms
topic.description: Legislative amendments and reforms to the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act (FISA) focusing on electronic surveillance, business records, and privacy protections.

Document 1: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to impose limits on roving electronic surveillance
Directs the AG to establish minimization and destruction procedures
Document 2: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to establish a new process for business records
Prohibits the FBI from applying for a tangible thing production order unless a specific selection term is used
Document 3: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to authorize the Attorney General and Director of National Intelligence to jointly authorize targeting
Requires the AG and DNI to assess compliance with required targeting and minimization procedures
Document 4: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to impose limits on roving electronic surveillance
Directs the AG to establish minimization and destruction procedures
Document 5: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to include the Privacy and Civil Liberties Oversight Board as a recipient of FISA court procedural rule changes
Reports on compliance with minimization procedures for targeting persons
Document 6: Provides standing for civil actions for declaratory and injunctive relief to persons who refrain from electronic communications through fear of being subject to warrantless electronic surveillance
Document 7: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to authorize an entity to make publicly available aggregate numbers regarding compliance with FISA orders
Includes descriptions of types of assistance required
Document 8: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 to authorize the FBI to apply for production of tangible things only if the subject is a specific person or group
Document 9: Reaffirms the authority of the Comptroller General to audit elements of the intelligence community
Requires cooperation from the intelligence community for audits
Document 10: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to state that surveillance directed at persons outside the US is not covered under electronic surveillance
Requires the AG to report to the Court on procedures for determining acquisitions

Document 2: Revises immigration- and alien-related provisions

Document 4: Gives state criminal alien assistance program funding priority
Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to complete the required 700 mile southwest border fencing
Document 5: Develop and submit to specified congressional committees a Northern Border Counternarcotics Strategy
Preventing the illegal trafficking of drugs across the international border
Document 8: Increase of full-time active-duty Border Patrol personnel along the southern border
Construct two layers of reinforced fencing along at least 150 miles of the southern border

topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at promoting renewable energy through tax credits and incentives.

Document 1: provide for the refundability of the residential energy efficiency tax credit for solar electric and water heating property
allow a refundable energy tax credit for investment in property used to manufacture solar energy property.
Document 2: extend through December 31, 2018, the income tax credit for new qualified fuel cell motor vehicles.
Document 3: extend through 2021 the residential energy efficient property tax credit for qualified fuel cell property
the energy tax credit for qualified fuel cell property
Document 4: allow an energy tax credit for highly efficient combined heat and power system property.
has a system design that provides an energy efficiency percentage of at least 70%
Document 5: extend through 2013 the tax credit for the production of electricity from wind facilities
the energy tax credit for investment in wind facilities
Document 6: provide tax incentives for investment in renewable energy sources
extending the tax credit for producing electricity from renewable resources
Document 7: revise the income and excise tax credits for biodiesel used as fuel
extends the biodiesel income and excise tax credits through December 31, 2014.
Document 9: establish a voluntary electric and thermal energy productivity challenge grant program
promote the goal of doubling electric and thermal energy productivity by January 1, 2030
Document 10: require a reduction in the tax credit for the production of electricity from renewable resources by grants and other credits
exempt certain loan guarantees or grants issued by the Secretary of Agriculture under the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002.

topic.name: Legislation on Military and Civilian Safety
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at ensuring the safety of civilians in relation to military actions and the management of federal resources.

Document 1: Places restrictions on the use of cluster munitions.
Directs the President to submit a plan for cleaning up hazardous munitions.

topic.name: Elder Care and Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative measures aimed at improving the care, protection, and rights of older adults, particularly in the context of abuse prevention, service delivery, and health care coordination.

Document 1: Younger-Onset Alzheimer's Disease Parity Act of 2016
authorize services to be provided to individuals with Alzheimer's disease who have not attained 60 years of age.
Document 2: Improving Care for Vulnerable Older Citizens through Workforce Advancement Act of 2014
focus on care coordination and service delivery for older individuals with chronic illness.
Document 3: Elder Protection and Abuse Prevention Act
ensure that all programs funded under such Act include appropriate training in elder abuse prevention.

topic.name: Agricultural Policy and Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to agricultural support programs, nutrition education, and assistance for veterans and disadvantaged farmers.

Document 1: Amends the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002 to authorize the Secretary of Agriculture to provide agricultural producers who produce bioenergy with commodity storage capacity loans.
Document 2: Amends the U.S. Troop Readiness, Veterans' Care, Katrina Recovery, and Iraq Accountability Appropriations Act, 2007 to eliminate the requirement that forage producers must participate in the insurance pilot program or the noninsured crop disaster assistance program in order to receive crop disaster and livestock assistance.
Document 3: Healthy Children Through School Nutrition Education Act - Amends the Child Nutrition and WIC Reauthorization Act of 2004 to require local educational agencies participating in the school lunch or breakfast programs to include in their school wellness policies the requirement that each student receive 50 hours of nutrition education each school year.
Document 4: Amends the Agricultural Risk Protection Act regarding the value-added agricultural product market development grants program to include farmers and ranchers who are veterans among priority recipients.
Document 5: Amends the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act to make foster children categorically eligible for free lunches and breakfasts under the school lunch and breakfast programs.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to provide cost-share payments to agricultural producers for water conservation projects on farms.

topic.name: Mortgage Regulation and Reform
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures related to mortgage financing, foreclosure prevention, and regulatory reforms in the housing market.

Document 1: Extends the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) demonstration projects on the guaranteeing of veterans' adjustable rate and hybrid adjustable rate mortgage loans.
similar to which the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development insures such mortgages under the National Housing Act.
Document 2: Prohibits a court, in the case of any federally related mortgage loan made after enactment of this Act, from entering a deficiency judgment requiring the mortgagor to pay any balance on the mortgage loan after a foreclosure sale of the mortgaged property.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to require specified mortgagees to indemnify HUD for payment of a mortgage insurance claim if the mortgage was not materially originated or underwritten in accordance with HUD requirements.

Authorizes the Secretary to require a mortgagee to indemnify HUD for loss regardless of when an insurance claim is paid if fraud or misrepresentation was involved in connection with the mortgage origination or underwriting.
Document 4: Amends the National Housing Act to prohibit each mortgagee (or servicer) from imposing, and the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) from requiring imposition of, any interest charge on an insured mortgage as a result of the loss of any time period provided by the mortgagee (or servicer) within which the mortgagor may fully repay the principal balance amount of the mortgage.
with respect to: (1) any days in the preceding billing cycle in which such amounts were repaid, or (2) any amounts repaid in the current billing cycle that were repaid within such time period.
Document 5: Appoints the Federal Housing Finance Agency (FHFA) receiver of the Federal National Mortgage Association (Fannie Mae) and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (Freddie Mac) (government sponsored enterprises or GSEs) and places them into irrevocable receivership.
Establishes the MFA as an independent agency of the federal government to: (1) guarantee securities issued by qualified issuers and collateralized by pools of qualified residential mortgages.
Document 6: Amends federal bankruptcy law governing a Chapter 13 debtor (adjustment of debts of an individual with regular income).
Allows modification of the rights of claim holders, in the event of a foreclosure notice for a chapter 13 debtor.
Document 7: Requires the Director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency to promulgate rules prohibiting the Federal National Mortgage Association (Fannie Mae) and Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (Freddie Mac) from holding any possessory interest in or otherwise owning, in whole or in part, any financial market instrument that would substantially reduce the financial incentive to carry out the statutory mission of the GSEs.
Directs Freddie Mac to implement policies to reduce the putback risk on a mortgage.
Document 8: Establishes in the legislative branch the Commission on the Foreclosure and Mortgage Lending Crisis to study and report to Congress on the current legal and regulatory framework governing the housing mortgage lending markets.
recommend changes to the current framework to prohibit lending practices that have contributed to the mortgage lending crisis.
Document 9: Amends the Truth in Lending Act with respect to requirements for disclosure to a consumer of points and fees information about a consumer credit transaction.
Excludes from the computation of such points and fees the amount of any loan level price adjustment payment set by government entities.
Document 10: Amends the National Housing Act of 1937 to modify the eligibility criteria for insurance of home equity conversion mortgages (reverse mortgages) of elderly homeowners.
Directs the Secretary to promulgate regulations to protect elderly homeowners from the marketing of financial and insurance products not in their interest.

topic.name: Designations of Public Facilities
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative designations of various public facilities, including post offices and federal buildings, across the United States.

Document 1: designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 175 South Monroe Street in Tiffin, Ohio, as the 'Paul E. Gillmor Post Office Building.'
Document 2: designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 20 Main Street in Little Ferry, New Jersey, as the 'Sergeant Matthew J. Fenton Post Office.'
Document 3: designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 2 Government Center in Fall River, Massachusetts, as the 'Sergeant Robert Barrett Post Office Building.'

Document 6: designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 1430 South Highway 29 in Cantonment, Florida, as the 'Charles H. Hendrix Post Office Building.'
Document 7: designates the federal building located at 2030 Southwest 145th Avenue in Miramar, Florida, as the 'Benjamin P. Grogan and Jerry L. Dove Federal Building.'
Document 8: designates the U.S. Federal Judicial Center located at 333 West Broadway Street in San Diego, California, as the 'John Rhoades Federal Judicial Center.'
Document 9: designates buildings occupied by the Department of Transportation located at 1200 New Jersey Avenue, Southeast, and 1201 4th Street, Southeast, in the District of Columbia as the James L. Oberstar United States Department of Transportation Building Complex.
Document 10: prohibits the United States Postal Service from closing any post office that does not have another post office within eight miles.

topic.description: Legislation aimed at promoting clean energy technologies, alternative fuels, and reducing greenhouse gas emissions in transportation.

Requires the estimate of cellulosic biofuel projected to be sold or introduced into commerce
Document 2: Requires the Department of Commerce to carry out a grant program for establishing consortia
Promoting domestic research, development, manufacture, and deployment of clean technologies
Document 3: Expands the tax credit for new qualified plug-in electric drive motor vehicles

Document 4: Allows an excise tax credit for alternative fuels and fuel mixtures
Extends the tax credit for alternative fuel vehicle refueling property expenditures
Document 5: Amends the Clean Air Act to apply limitations on Reid vapor pressure
Applicable to gasoline blended with more than 10% ethanol
Document 6: Requires the Department of Transportation to designate Clean Vehicle Corridors
Encourages the addition of cleaner alternative fuel options
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Energy to conduct a program of basic and applied research
On materials, technologies, and processes with the potential to reduce petroleum use
Document 8: Establishes a national energy security program to reduce oil consumption
Directs the President to develop a national oil independence plan
Document 9: Establishes a program to provide loan guarantees for advanced vehicle batteries
Encourages the use of plug-in electric drive vehicles
Document 10: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to expand the tax credit for new qualified plug-in electric drive motor vehicles
Includes vehicles powered by an alternative electrical energy storage device other than a battery

topic.name: Health Care Legislation and Reforms
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative amendments and acts related to health care policies, insurance coverage, and the management of health care programs in the United States.

Document 1: Repeals the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act (PPACA) and the Health Care and Education Reconciliation Act of 2010
Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow the tax deduction for medical expenses without the gross income percentage limitation.
Document 2: Amends the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to transfer from the Prevention and Public Health Fund
Eliminates the eligibility requirement for the temporary high risk pool.
Document 3: Amends the Public Health Service Act to allow a health insurance issuer to impose a preexisting condition exclusion
Individual health insurance coverage only to the extent the issuer may impose such exclusion with respect to group health insurance coverage.
Document 4: Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 to prohibit a group health plan from imposing any preexisting condition exclusion for individuals who have not attained 25 years of age
Applies such requirement to coverage offered in the individual market.
Document 5: Amends the Public Health Service Act to provide that the laws of the state designated by a health insurance issuer shall apply to individual health insurance coverage
Prohibits an issuer from offering, selling, or issuing individual health insurance coverage in a secondary state if the state insurance commissioner does not use a risk-based capital formula.
Document 6: Amends the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act to allow states to apply for a waiver of specified requirements
Requires the Secretary of HHS or the Treasury to reconsider the determination in the event of an appeal.
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to modify the definition of 'applicable large employer'
Excludes from such definition a small employer that is eligible to purchase a qualified health plan.
Document 8: Amends Internal Revenue Code provisions relating to health savings accounts
Allows Medicare beneficiaries participating in a Medicare Advantage medical savings account to exclude from gross income payments made to their MSAs.
Document 9: Amends the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act to prohibit anything in the Act from being construed to require an individual to terminate coverage
Allows continuation of coverage under a group health plan.
Document 10: Amends the Public Health Service Act, the Employee Retirement Income Security Act, and the Internal Revenue Code to require a group health plan to provide coverage for routine HIV screening
Applies such requirements to health insurance coverage offered in the individual market.

topic.name: Oil and Gas Leasing Regulations
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative acts and amendments related to the regulation, leasing, and production of oil and gas resources in the United States.

1: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to issue regulations to establish a specified graduated annual production incentive fee with respect to federal onshore and offshore lands subject to a lease for production of oil or natural gas under which production is not occurring.

This bill directs the Department of the Interior to issue regulations to establish a specified graduated annual production incentive fee with respect to federal onshore and offshore lands subject to a lease for production of oil or natural gas under which production is not occurring.

4: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to agree to a request by a lessee to amend any lease issued for any Central and Western Gulf of Mexico tract between January 1, 1998, and January 1, 2000, to incorporate price thresholds applicable to royalty suspension provisions.

Prohibits the Secretary from issuing a new lease to certain persons or entities with any direct or indirect interest in, or who derive any benefit from, a covered lease unless they renegotiate the lease to include such price thresholds or have paid or formally agreed to pay all conservation of resources fees established under this Act.
5: Amounts received for the fee shall be reserved for expenditures on inspection, enforcement, and permitting relating to oil and gas.
6: Declares without force or effect all federal prohibitions against the expenditure of appropriated funds to conduct natural gas leasing and preleasing activities for any area of the Outer Continental Shelf (OCS).

Prohibits the Secretary from issuing a lease for natural gas exploration or development in any OCS area located more than 50 miles and less than 100 miles from the coastline of a state if the state has enacted a law disapproving such lease issuance.

topic.name: Regulatory Impact and Accountability

Document 6: Defines the 'economic impact' of a rule to include any direct or indirect economic effects on small entities

Document 8: Require regulatory impact analyses for rules that do not involve a legislative mandate

topic.name: Consumer Credit Protection
topic.description: Legislation aimed at safeguarding consumers in their use of credit cards and other financial products.

Document 1: Prepaid Card Consumer Protection Act of 2013 - Amends the Electronic Fund Transfer Act to extend its coverage to spending accounts established by a consumer.
Treats as a spending account any similar deposit account operated or managed by a financial institution.
Document 2: Stop Unfair Practices in Credit Cards Act of 2007 - Amends the Truth in Lending Act regarding open-end consumer credit plans.
Prohibits imposition of an interest charge upon debt paid on time and in full.
Document 3: Electronic Funds Transfer Equal Consumer Protection Act - Amends the Electronic Fund Transfer Act to treat as an error any electronic fund transfer affecting the customer's account.

Document 4: Small Business Credit Card Act of 2013 - Amends the Truth in Lending Act to cover any qualified small business as a consumer with respect to a credit card account.
Prohibits a creditor from discriminating against any business concern having 50 or fewer employees in connection with any credit card account.
Document 5: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require any person selling personal property to consumers on a business premises to display conspicuously a sign that contains the same information required by the Act.

topic.name: Labor Relations and Employee Rights
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures related to labor relations, employee rights, and the role of the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) in collective bargaining processes.

Document 1: Amends the National Labor Relations Act (NLRA) to make it an unlawful labor practice for a labor organization or its agents to interfere with the rights of employees to organize and select representation to bargain collectively.

Requires the NLRB to decide, before the election of a labor organization as the exclusive collective bargaining representative of all employees of an appropriate unit, whether such unit shall be the employer unit, craft unit, plant unit, or subdivision unit.
Document 2: Declares that any agency action taken by the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) since January 4, 2012, shall be void unless each NLRB member has been appointed: (1) by and with the advice and consent of the Senate, or (2) by the President to fill a vacancy during a recess of the Senate.
Prohibits the NLRB from taking any agency action until final disposition is made in all such actions.
Document 3: This bill amends the National Labor Relations Act to authorize a person alleging an unfair labor practice by an employer by discrimination regarding hiring or tenure of employment or any term or condition of employment to encourage or discourage membership in any labor organization.

The court may grant relief (including injunctive and equitable relief, back pay, and compensatory and punitive damages) and allow the prevailing party a reasonable attorney's fee as part of the costs.
Document 5: This bill repeals current authority allowing the deduction of labor organization dues from employee pay and prohibits federal agencies... from deducting any amount from the pay of an employee for the dues of a labor organization.
An employee may elect, in writing, to withhold personal information from a labor organization during organizing activity.
Document 6: Amends the National Labor Relations Act (NLRA) to revise requirements for determination by the National Labor Relation Board (NLRB) of an appropriate bargaining unit before an election of collective bargaining representation.

Requires the NLRB to: (1) direct an election by secret ballot as soon as practicable, but in any event not before 35 calendar days following the filing of an election petition, in cases where a question of representation exists.
Document 7: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to make it an unfair labor practice for: (1) an employer to recognize or bargain collectively with a labor organization that has not been selected by a majority of the employees in a unit appropriate for such purposes in a secret ballot election.

Requires the NLRB to conduct a secret ballot election to determine whether a labor organization certified or recognized by an employer as the representative to bargain collectively is no longer the representative of a unit.

topic.name: Campaign Finance Reform
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative efforts to reform campaign finance laws in the United States, particularly amendments to the Federal Election Campaign Act (FECA) aimed at increasing transparency and regulating contributions and expenditures in federal elections.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 (FECA) to terminate limitations on federal election campaign contributions after 2007.
Requires electronic filing of all reports, and their placement on the Internet.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a refundable credit of 50% of qualified congressional House campaign contributions.
Directs the Government by the People Oversight Commission to launch a pilot program under which it shall select three eligible states to operate a voucher pilot program.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 (FECA) to set forth specified expenditure limitations for House of Representatives general elections.
Establishes in the Treasury the Grassroots Good Citizenship Fund (Fund), which shall provide public funding for House candidates' expenditures.
Document 4: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 (FECA) to allow the funds of a leadership PAC to be used for otherwise authorized expenditures in connection with campaigns for election for federal office.
Defines leadership PAC as a political committee which is directly or indirectly established, maintained, or controlled by a candidate for election for federal office.
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to permit a tax-exempt organization to make certain statements related to a political campaign without losing its tax-exempt status.
An organization may not lose its tax-exempt status under section 501(c)(3) solely because of the content of any statement made in the ordinary course of the organization's regular activities.
Document 6: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 (FECA) with respect to eligibility and qualifying contribution requirements for clean election financing of House election campaigns.
Establishes the House Clean Elections Fund.
Document 7: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution of $1,000 or more to the Federal Election Commission (FEC).
Requires Senate candidates to file designations, statements, and reports directly with the FEC.
Document 8: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 (FECA) to prohibit independent expenditures and payments for electioneering communications by government contractors if the value of the contract is at least $10 million.
Declares that nothing prohibits any domestic corporation from establishing, administering, and soliciting contributions to a separate segregated fund.
Document 9: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 and the Internal Revenue Code to set forth requirements for the disclosure of independent federal election-related contributions and expenditures.

Defines 'independent federal election-related activity expenditure' to mean any expenditure that a reasonable person would conclude is made solely or substantially to influence or attempt to influence the nomination or election of any individual to federal office.
Document 10: Amends the Internal Revenue Code and the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the system of public financing for presidential primary and general elections occurring after January 1, 2016.
Increases the amount of matching funds for presidential primaries from a one-to-one match to a six-to-one match for contributions of $250 or less from individuals.

Requires the Secretary to provide institutions of higher education and states with that tax return information, without charge.
Document 7: Prohibits the Secretary of the Treasury from requiring a payor of interest to file an information return on interest that is not effectively connected with a trade or business within the United States.
that is paid to a nonresident alien on a deposit maintained at an office within the United States.
Document 8: Tax Refund Protection Act of 2015 - This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to direct the Department of the Treasury to establish a program to license or certify and regulate tax return preparers.
The program must require that the tax return preparer demonstrate good character, good reputation, necessary qualifications.
Document 9: Small Business Taxpayer Bill of Rights Act of 2012 - Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow businesses with average annual gross receipts of not more than $50,000 that prevail in an administrative or court proceeding involving the determination, collection, or refund of tax, interest, or penalty to recover their costs incurred in such proceedings.
Prohibits ex parte communications between officers in the IRS Office of Appeals and other IRS employees.
Document 10: IRS Abuse Protection Act of 2013 - Amends the Internal Revenue Code to require the Secretary of the Treasury to provide written notice to a taxpayer any time such taxpayer's account, tax return, or return information is accessed by the Department of the Treasury.

topic.name: Guantanamo Bay Detention Policies
topic.description: The documents discuss U.S. legislation and policies regarding the detention, treatment, and legal rights of individuals held at Guantanamo Bay, Cuba.

Document 10: Prohibits the President from releasing or transferring an individual currently detained at Naval Station, Guantanamo Bay, Cuba, to the custody of the individual's country of origin.
Requires certification to Congress regarding the security of the country before any transfer.

redirect resources that are currently being used for nuclear weapons programs to constructive, ecologically beneficial peacetime activities.

promulgate regulations for facilities and materials used in thorium fuel cycle nuclear power generation.

Document 10: Amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to revise terms and conditions governing federal loan guarantees for nuclear technology projects.
